title: Contact
text:
ALS coverage data sources

The table below lists the coverage sources by jurisdiction. Last update is the date when the jurisdictional coverage layer in this project was refreshed; it is not the date of the most recent ALS acquisition.

{r, echo=F, warning=F, message=F} tribble(   ~Jurisdiction, ~`Data source`,   "BC", "BC Ministry of Forests and [lidar.gov.bc.ca](https://lidar.gov.bc.ca/).",   "AB", "AB Forestry and Parks, Alberta Biodiversity Monitoring Institute (ABMI)",   "SK", "SK Ministry of Environment",   "ON", "Ministry of Natural Resources",   "QC", "Ministere des Ressources naturelles et des Forets, [donneesquebec.ca/recherche/dataset/donnees-lidar-du-quebec](https://www.donneesquebec.ca/recherche/dataset/donnees-lidar-du-quebec)",   "NB", "NB Natural Resources and Energy Development, and [www.snb.ca/geonb1](http://www.snb.ca/geonb1/e/dc/catalogue-E.asp)",   "PE", "Prince Edward Island Department of Environment, Energy and Climate Action",   "NS", "[nsgi.novascotia.ca/datalocator](https://nsgi.novascotia.ca/datalocator/elevation/) and Department of Natural Resources and Renewables" ) %>%   left_join(tbl_updates) %>%   relocate(`Data source`, .after = last_col()) %>%   kable()

title: Contact
text:
ALS data availability
{r, echo=F, warning=F, message=F} tribble(   ~Jurisdiction, ~`Data sharing policy`,   "BC", "ALS - open data.<br>DEM and CHM from systematic provincial acquisition will be shared. <br>[www.lidar.gov.bc.ca](https://www.lidar.gov.bc.ca)",   "AB", "GoA: Initial provincial coverage ALS data and derivatives were acquired with a licence (i.e., data were leased for 99 years). Sharing is limited to GoA partners (e.g. academia) for collaborative projects and forest industry. <br>Industry: 11 projects between 2017 and 2022 funded through the Forest Resource Improvement Program (FRIP). Data are not currently open, although FRIP is public funds. <br>ABMI acquisitions [will be made freely available](https://abmi.ca/home/news-events/news/Open-Lidar-Data-Announcement.html?mode=detail&scroll).",   "SK", "ALS point clouds currently not shared as open data.<br>Derivatives (DEM) are shared openly (not all derived from lidar): [environment-saskatchewan.hub.arcgis.com](https://environment-saskatchewan.hub.arcgis.com/pages/forest_resource_inventory)",   "ON", "ALS point clouds and derivatives (DEM, CHM, DSM) are open data, available at [geohub.lio.gov.on.ca](https://geohub.lio.gov.on.ca/maps/lio::forest-resources-inventory-leaf-on-lidar/about)",   "QC", "ALS - open data: [donneesquebec.ca/recherche/dataset/donnees-lidar-du-quebec](https://www.donneesquebec.ca/recherche/dataset/donnees-lidar-du-quebec)",   "NB", "ALS data are open and available for download: [www.snb.ca/geonb1/e/dc/lidar.asp](http://www.snb.ca/geonb1/e/dc/lidar.asp).<br>Derivatives (i.e. DEM, CHM, hillshade, slope) are open and are available as [WMS/WCS service](https://www2.gnb.ca/content/gnb/en/departments/erd/open-data/raster-data.html#12).",   "PE", "ALS data available for download: [download-telecharger.services.geo.ca](https://download-telecharger.services.geo.ca/pub/elevation/pointclouds_nuagespoints/PEI/)",   "NS", "ALS files and derivatives are open data ([nsgi.novascotia.ca/datalocator/elevation/](https://nsgi.novascotia.ca/datalocator/elevation/))." ) |>   knitr::kable(escape = FALSE)

    Resources
    Update log
    Coverage additions are logged here as dated map snapshots. This keeps the refresh history visible without overloading the landing page.
  


```{r, echo=F, message=F} library(tidyverse) library(lubridate)
img_folder <- “img/UpdateLog” img_files <- list.files(path = img_folder, pattern = “\.png$”, full.names = TRUE)
img_info <- tibble(file = img_files) %>% mutate( filename = basename(file), date_str = str_extract(filename, “\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}”), date = ymd(date_str) ) %>% arrange(desc(date))
output <- map2_chr( img_info\(file,
  img_info\)date, ~ glue::glue(“## ALS coverage added on {format(.y, ‘%B %d, %Y’)}{{width=100%}}”) )
knitr::asis_output(paste(output, collapse = “”))

```{=html}
</div>

    Use this page to report missing coverage, flag errors, or point us to jurisdictional updates that should be reflected in the maps.
  



If you find errors or know of ALS coverage that should be added to the maps, please open an issue in the repository or contact the project team directly.



GitHub issues
Report a problem or suggest a correction through the project issue tracker.


Direct contacts
Piotr Tompalski: piotr.tompalski@nrcan-rncan.gc.ca
Joanne White: joanne.white@nrcan-rncan.gc.ca

title: Data

ALS coverage data sources

The table below lists the coverage sources by jurisdiction. Last update is the date when the jurisdictional coverage layer in this project was refreshed; it is not the date of the most recent ALS acquisition.






Jurisdiction
Last update
Data source

BC Ministry of Forests and lidar.gov.bc.ca.


AB

title: Data
text:
ALS data availability





Jurisdiction
Data sharing policy




BC
ALS - open data.DEM and CHM from systematic provincial acquisition will be shared. www.lidar.gov.bc.ca


AB
GoA: Initial provincial coverage ALS data and derivatives were acquired with a licence (i.e., data were leased for 99 years). Sharing is limited to GoA partners (e.g. academia) for collaborative projects and forest industry. Industry: 11 projects between 2017 and 2022 funded through the Forest Resource Improvement Program (FRIP). Data are not currently open, although FRIP is public funds. ABMI acquisitions will be made freely available.


SK
ALS point clouds currently not shared as open data.Derivatives (DEM) are shared openly (not all derived from lidar): environment-saskatchewan.hub.arcgis.com


ON
ALS point clouds and derivatives (DEM, CHM, DSM) are open data, available at geohub.lio.gov.on.ca


QC
ALS - open data: donneesquebec.ca/recherche/dataset/donnees-lidar-du-quebec


NB
ALS data are open and available for download: www.snb.ca/geonb1/e/dc/lidar.asp.Derivatives (i.e. DEM, CHM, hillshade, slope) are open and are available as WMS/WCS service.


PE
ALS data available for download: download-telecharger.services.geo.ca


NS
ALS files and derivatives are open data (nsgi.novascotia.ca/datalocator/elevation/).

title: ALS data coverage in Canadian forests
text:
Summary

The national overview is supported by jurisdiction-specific source checks and periodic refreshes. The table below summarizes the current ALS coverage figures used across the site, followed by cumulative acquisition area through time.


Coverage by jurisdiction










Jurisdiction
Total ALS coverage
ALS coverage over managed forest




British Columbia (BC)
391,123 (42.3%)
268,103 (53.7%)


Alberta (AB)
395,121 (61.5%)
236,209 (94.5%)


Saskatchewan (SK)
108,660 (18.4%)
69,555 (81%)


Ontario (ON)
445,042 (48.5%)
333,744 (99.6%)


Quebec (QC)
551,196 (40.4%)
375,949 (98.8%)


New Brunswick (NB)
74,795 (100%)
58,299 (100%)


Prince Edward Island (PE)
5,734 (100%)
2,429 (100%)


Nova Scotia (NS)
56,684 (100%)
37,279 (100%)







Coverage over time









Related publication
Enhanced forest inventories in Canada: implementation, status, and research needs
This website extends the published national overview with updated coverage sources, current summary statistics, and interactive map products that continue to evolve beyond the paper snapshot.
White, J.C., Tompalski, P., Bater, C.W., Wulder, M.A., Fortin, M., Hennigar, C., Robere-McGugan, G., Sinclair, I., White, R. (2025). Enhanced forest inventories in Canada: implementation, status, and research needs. Canadian Journal of Forest Research, 55: 1-37.

Read the paper

title: ALS data coverage in Canadian forests
text:
Data sources

Coverage sources and availability
The jurisdictional coverage layers in this compilation come from a mix of public download services and direct exchanges with provincial organizations responsible for ALS data management. Last update is the date when the jurisdictional coverage layer in this project was refreshed, not the date of the latest ALS acquisition.






Jurisdiction
Last update
Source and sharing




British Columbia (BC)
2026-04-21
Source: BC Ministry of Forests and lidar.gov.bc.ca.Access: ALS open data. DEM and CHM from systematic provincial acquisition will be shared. www.lidar.gov.bc.ca


Alberta (AB)
2025-10-15
Source: AB Forestry and Parks, Alberta Biodiversity Monitoring Institute (ABMI).Access: Initial provincial coverage ALS data and derivatives were acquired with a licence. Sharing is limited to GoA partners for collaborative projects and forest industry. FRIP-funded industry projects are not currently open. ABMI acquisitions will be made freely available.


Saskatchewan (SK)
2025-07-17
Source: SK Ministry of Environment.Access: ALS point clouds are not currently shared as open data. Derivatives (DEM) are shared openly, though not all are lidar-derived: environment-saskatchewan.hub.arcgis.com.


Ontario (ON)
2026-04-21
Source: Ministry of Natural Resources.Access: ALS point clouds and derivatives (DEM, CHM, DSM) are open data, available at geohub.lio.gov.on.ca.


Quebec (QC)
2025-10-28
Source: Ministere des Ressources naturelles et des Forets, donneesquebec.ca/recherche/dataset/donnees-lidar-du-quebec.Access: ALS open data: donneesquebec.ca/recherche/dataset/donnees-lidar-du-quebec.


New Brunswick (NB)
2026-04-22
Source: NB Natural Resources and Energy Development, and www.snb.ca/geonb1.Access: ALS data are open and available for download: www.snb.ca/geonb1/e/dc/lidar.asp. Derivatives (DEM, CHM, hillshade, slope) are also available as WMS/WCS service.


Prince Edward Island (PE)
2024-07-18
Source: Prince Edward Island Department of Environment, Energy and Climate Action.Access: ALS data available for download: download-telecharger.services.geo.ca.


Nova Scotia (NS)
2025-07-17
Source: nsgi.novascotia.ca/datalocator and Department of Natural Resources and Renewables.Access: ALS files and derivatives are open data (nsgi.novascotia.ca/datalocator/elevation/).






Additional ALS datasets in Canada


High Resolution Digital Elevation Model (HRDEM) - CanElevation Series


High Resolution Digital Elevation Model Mosaic (HRDEM Mosaic) CanElevation Series


National Elevation Data Strategy


LiDAR Point Clouds - CanElevation Series


Petawawa Research Forest
